title: Arthur the King Box Office Performance Analysis
text: Lionsgate's Arthur the King earned $7.5 million during its opening weekend, falling below expectations of $8-10 million. The Mark Wahlberg adventure film, which cost $19 million to produce, underperformed at 3,003 theaters. The film received strong audience approval with an A CinemaScore and high PostTrak scores of 4.5 stars with 75% definite recommendation. On Rotten Tomatoes, audiences rated it 93-97% fresh, though critics were more measured at 63%. Key demographics include nearly even split between male and female audiences, with 36% of ticket buyers aged 18-34. The largest demographic consisted of 25-34 year-olds (23%) and those over 55 (22%). Women over 25 gave strongest ratings at 90%. Diversity composition was 52% Caucasian, 25% Latino/Hispanic, 8% Black, 10% Asian. The movie performed worse than comparable titles. Channing Tatum's similar dog-adventure film Dog opened to $14.8 million in February 2022. Industry observers noted the film's Canada box office was unavailable due to a Prime Video streaming window, though including those numbers would only push the opening to approximately $8.5 million.
